title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs quorum queues, raft replication, availability, and data safety from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the quorum queue safety and availability decision record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for quorum queue safety and availability decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Quorum Use Case Fit
steps.instruction:
Order the Quorum Use Case Fit RabbitMQ and TypeScript messaging evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Quorum Use Case Fit.

content.text: A review messaging team handles case 1383 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
